001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.time;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.StateEnum.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021
022import java.time.*;
023import java.time.format.*;
024import java.time.temporal.*;
025import java.util.*;
026
027import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
028
029/**
030 * A ZonedDateTime with precision information for granular time operations.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * This class combines a {@link ZonedDateTime} with a {@link ChronoField} precision identifier,
034 * allowing for granular time operations such as rolling by specific time units.
035 *
036 * <p>
037 * The precision field indicates the granularity of the time value, which determines how the
038 * {@link #roll(int)} method behaves. For example, a precision of {@link ChronoField#YEAR} means
039 * rolling by 1 will advance the year, while a precision of {@link ChronoField#HOUR_OF_DAY} means
040 * rolling by 1 will advance the hour.
041 *
042 * <h5 class='section'>ISO8601 Parsing:</h5>
043 * <p>
044 * The {@link #of(String)} method can parse various ISO8601 timestamp formats:
045 * <ul>
046 *    <li>Date formats: <js>"2011"</js>, <js>"2011-01"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15"</js>
047 *    <li>DateTime formats: <js>"2011-01-15T12"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45"</js>
048 *    <li>With fractional seconds: <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45.123"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45,123"</js>
049 *    <li>Time-only formats: <js>"T12"</js>, <js>"T12:30"</js>, <js>"T12:30:45"</js>
050 *    <li>With timezone: <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45Z"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45+05:30"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45-05:30"</js>
051 * </ul>
052 *
053 * <p>
054 * The precision is automatically determined from the input format. For example:
055 * <ul>
056 *    <li><js>"2011"</js> → {@link ChronoField#YEAR}
057 *    <li><js>"2011-01"</js> → {@link ChronoField#MONTH_OF_YEAR}
058 *    <li><js>"2011-01-15"</js> → {@link ChronoField#DAY_OF_MONTH}
059 *    <li><js>"2011-01-15T12"</js> → {@link ChronoField#HOUR_OF_DAY}
060 *    <li><js>"2011-01-15T12:30"</js> → {@link ChronoField#MINUTE_OF_HOUR}
061 *    <li><js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45"</js> → {@link ChronoField#SECOND_OF_MINUTE}
062 *    <li><js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45.123"</js> → {@link ChronoField#MILLI_OF_SECOND} (1-3 digits)
063 *    <li><js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45.123456789"</js> → {@link ChronoField#NANO_OF_SECOND} (4-9 digits)
064 * </ul>
065 *
066 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
067 * <p class='bjava'>
068 *    <jc>// Parse an ISO8601 timestamp with year precision</jc>
069 *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"2011"</js>);
070 *    <jc>// Roll forward by one year</jc>
071 *    <jv>gdt</jv> = <jv>gdt</jv>.<jsm>roll</jsm>(1);
072 *    <jc>// Get the ZonedDateTime</jc>
073 *    ZonedDateTime <jv>zdt</jv> = <jv>gdt</jv>.<jsm>getZonedDateTime</jsm>();
074 *    <jc>// Result: 2012-01-01T00:00:00 with system default timezone</jc>
075 * </p>
076 *
077 * <p class='bjava'>
078 *    <jc>// Parse a datetime with hour precision</jc>
079 *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt2</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"2011-01-15T12Z"</js>);
080 *    <jc>// Roll forward by 2 hours</jc>
081 *    <jv>gdt2</jv> = <jv>gdt2</jv>.<jsm>roll</jsm>(<jv>ChronoField</jv>.<jf>HOUR_OF_DAY</jf>, 2);
082 *    <jc>// Result: 2011-01-15T14:00:00Z</jc>
083 * </p>
084 *
085 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
086 * <p>
087 * This class is immutable and thread-safe.
088 *
089 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
090 *    <li class='jm'>{@link ZonedDateTime}
091 *    <li class='jm'>{@link ChronoField}
092 *    <li class='jm'>{@link DateUtils}
093 * </ul>
094 */
095@SuppressWarnings({
096   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
097})
098public class GranularZonedDateTime {
099
100   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
101   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
102
103   /**
104    * Creates a GranularZonedDateTime from a Date with the specified precision.
105    *
106    * <p>
107    * The date is converted to a ZonedDateTime using the system default timezone.
108    *
109    * @param date The date to convert.
110    * @param precision The precision of the time value.
111    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime instance.
112    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if date or precision is null.
113    */
114   public static GranularZonedDateTime of(Date date, ChronoField precision) {
115      return of(date, precision, ZoneId.systemDefault());
116   }
117
118   /**
119    * Creates a GranularZonedDateTime from a Date with the specified precision and timezone.
120    *
121    * <p>
122    * The date is converted to a ZonedDateTime using the specified timezone.
123    *
124    * @param date The date to convert.
125    * @param precision The precision of the time value.
126    * @param zoneId The timezone to use.
127    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime instance.
128    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if date, precision, or zoneId is null.
129    */
130   public static GranularZonedDateTime of(Date date, ChronoField precision, ZoneId zoneId) {
131      return of(date.toInstant().atZone(zoneId), precision);
132   }
133
134   /**
135    * Parses an ISO8601 timestamp string into a GranularZonedDateTime.
136    *
137    * <p>
138    * This method parses various ISO8601 formats and automatically determines the precision
139    * based on the format. If no timezone is specified in the string, the system default
140    * timezone is used.
141    *
142    * <h5 class='section'>Supported Formats:</h5>
143    * <ul>
144    *    <li>Date: <js>"2011"</js>, <js>"2011-01"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15"</js>
145    *    <li>DateTime: <js>"2011-01-15T12"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45"</js>
146    *    <li>With fractional seconds: <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45.123"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45,123"</js>
147    *    <li>Time-only: <js>"T12"</js>, <js>"T12:30"</js>, <js>"T12:30:45"</js> (uses current date)
148    *    <li>With timezone: <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45Z"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45+05:30"</js>, <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45-05:30"</js>
149    * </ul>
150    *
151    * <h5 class='section'>Timezone Handling:</h5>
152    * <ul>
153    *    <li>If the string contains a timezone (Z, +HH:mm, -HH:mm, etc.), that timezone is used.
154    *    <li>If no timezone is specified, the system default timezone is used.
155    *    <li>For time-only formats (starting with "T"), the current date is used with the specified or default timezone.
156    * </ul>
157    *
158    * <h5 class='section'>Precision Detection:</h5>
159    * <p>
160    * The precision is automatically determined from the format:
161    * <ul>
162    *    <li>Year only → {@link ChronoField#YEAR}
163    *    <li>Year-Month → {@link ChronoField#MONTH_OF_YEAR}
164    *    <li>Year-Month-Day → {@link ChronoField#DAY_OF_MONTH}
165    *    <li>With hour → {@link ChronoField#HOUR_OF_DAY}
166    *    <li>With minute → {@link ChronoField#MINUTE_OF_HOUR}
167    *    <li>With second → {@link ChronoField#SECOND_OF_MINUTE}
168    *    <li>With 1-3 fractional digits → {@link ChronoField#MILLI_OF_SECOND}
169    *    <li>With 4-9 fractional digits → {@link ChronoField#NANO_OF_SECOND}
170    * </ul>
171    *
172    * @param value The ISO8601 timestamp string to parse.
173    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime instance.
174    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if timestamp is null.
175    * @throws DateTimeParseException if the timestamp format is invalid.
176    */
177   public static GranularZonedDateTime of(String value) {
178      return of(value, null, null);
179   }
180
181   /**
182    * Parses an ISO8601 timestamp string into a GranularZonedDateTime with a custom clock.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * This method is similar to {@link #of(String)}, but allows you to specify a custom
186    * {@link Clock} to use for obtaining the time zone and current time.
187    * This is useful for testing or when you need deterministic time behavior.
188    *
189    * <p>
190    * The clock is used when:
191    * <ul>
192    *    <li>No timezone is specified in the string - uses {@link Clock#getZone()}
193    *    <li>Time-only formats (starting with "T") - uses the clock's instant to get the current date
194    * </ul>
195    *
196    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
197    * <p class='bjava'>
198    *    <jc>// Parse with a fixed clock for deterministic testing</jc>
199    *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>clock</jv> = Clock.<jsm>fixed</jsm>(Instant.<jsm>parse</jsm>(<js>"2000-01-01T12:00:00Z"</js>), ZoneOffset.<jsf>UTC</jsf>);
200    *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(
201    *       <js>"T12:30:45"</js>,
202    *       <jv>clock</jv>
203    *    );
204    *    <jc>// Result uses the clock's current date and timezone</jc>
205    * </p>
206    *
207    * @param value The ISO8601 timestamp string to parse.
208    * @param clock The clock to use for time zone and current time.
209    *    If null, {@link Clock#systemDefaultZone()} is used.
210    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime instance.
211    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if value is null.
212    * @throws DateTimeParseException if the timestamp format is invalid.
213    */
214   public static GranularZonedDateTime of(String value, Clock clock) {
215      return of(value, null, clock);
216   }
217
218
219   /**
220    * Parses an ISO8601 timestamp string into a GranularZonedDateTime with a default timezone and custom clock.
221    *
222    * <p>
223    * This method is similar to {@link #of(String)}, but allows you to specify both a default
224    * timezone and a custom {@link Clock} to use when no timezone is present in the timestamp string.
225    *
226    * <p>
227    * If the timestamp string contains a timezone (Z, +HH:mm, -HH:mm, etc.), that timezone
228    * takes precedence over the defaultZoneId parameter. The defaultZoneId is only used when
229    * no timezone is specified in the string.
230    *
231    * <p>
232    * The clock is used when:
233    * <ul>
234    *    <li>No timezone is specified and defaultZoneId is null - uses {@link Clock#getZone()}
235    *    <li>Time-only formats (starting with "T") - uses the clock's instant to get the current date
236    * </ul>
237    *
238    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
239    * <p class='bjava'>
240    *    <jc>// Parse with default timezone and a fixed clock</jc>
241    *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>clock</jv> = Clock.<jsm>fixed</jsm>(Instant.<jsm>parse</jsm>(<js>"2000-01-01T12:00:00Z"</js>), ZoneOffset.<jsf>UTC</jsf>);
242    *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt1</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(
243    *       <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45"</js>,
244    *       <jv>ZoneId</jv>.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"America/New_York"</js>),
245    *       <jv>clock</jv>
246    *    );
247    *    <jc>// Result uses America/New_York timezone</jc>
248    *
249    *    <jc>// Parse with timezone in string (defaultZoneId is ignored)</jc>
250    *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt2</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(
251    *       <js>"2011-01-15T12:30:45Z"</js>,
252    *       <jv>ZoneId</jv>.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"America/New_York"</js>),
253    *       <jv>clock</jv>
254    *    );
255    *    <jc>// Result uses UTC (Z), not America/New_York</jc>
256    * </p>
257    *
258    * @param value The ISO8601 timestamp string to parse.
259    * @param defaultZoneId The default timezone to use if no timezone is specified in the string.
260    *    If null, the clock's time zone is used.
261    * @param clock The clock to use for time zone and current time.
262    *    If null, {@link Clock#systemDefaultZone()} is used.
263    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime instance.
264    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if value is null.
265    * @throws DateTimeParseException if the timestamp format is invalid.
266    */
267   
268   @SuppressWarnings({
269      "java:S6541", // Single-threaded context; synchronization unnecessary
270      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
271   })
272   public static GranularZonedDateTime of(String value, ZoneId defaultZoneId, Clock clock) {
273      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
274      var digit = StringUtils.DIGIT;
275      clock = clock == null ? Clock.systemDefaultZone() : clock;
276
277      // States:
278      // S01: Looking for Y(S02) or T(S07).
279      // S02: Found Y, looking for Y(S02)/-(S03)/T(S07).
280      // S03: Found -, looking for M(S04).
281      // S04: Found M, looking for M(S04)/-(S05)/T(S07).
282      // S05: Found -, looking for D(S10).
283      // S06  Found D, looking for D(S06)/T(S07).
284      // S07: Found T, looking for h(S08)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
285      // S08: Found h, looking for h(S08)/:(S09)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
286      // S09: Found :, looking for m(S10).
287      // S10: Found m, looking for m(S10)/:(S11)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
288      // S11: Found :, looking for s(S12).
289      // S12: Found s, looking for s(S12)/.(S13)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
290      // S13: Found ., looking for S(S14)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
291      // S14: Found S, looking for S(S14)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
292      // S15: Found Z.
293      // S16: Found +, looking for oh(S18).
294      // S17: Found -, looking for oh(S18).
295      // S18: Found oh, looking for oh(S18)/:(S19).
296      // S19: Found :, looking for om(S20).
297      // S20: Found om, looking for om(S20).
298
299
300      int year = 1;
301      int month = 1;
302      int day = 1;
303      int hour = 0;
304      int minute = 0;
305      int second = 0;
306      int nanos = 0;
307      int ohour = -1;
308      int ominute = -1;
309      boolean nego = false; // negative offset
310      boolean timeOnly = false; // Track if format started with "T" (time-only)
311      ZoneId zoneId = null;
312      var state = S1;
313      var mark = 0;
314      ChronoField precision = ChronoField.YEAR; // Track precision as we go
315
316      for (var i = 0; i < value.length(); i++) {
317         var c = value.charAt(i);
318
319         if (state == S1) {
320            // S01: Looking for Y(S02) or T(S07)
321            if (digit.contains(c)) {
322               mark = i;
323               state = S2;
324            } else if (c == 'T') {
325               timeOnly = true; // Mark as time-only format
326               state = S7;
327            } else {
328               throw bad(value, i);
329            }
330         } else if (state == S2) {
331            // S02: Found Y, looking for Y(S02)/-(S03)/T(S07)
332            if (digit.contains(c)) {
333               // Stay in S2
334            } else if (c == '-') {
335               year = parse(value, 4, mark, i, 0, 9999);
336               state = S3;
337            } else if (c == 'T') {
338               year = parse(value, 4, mark, i, 0, 9999);
339               state = S7;
340            } else if (c == 'Z') {
341               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
342               year = parse(value, 4, mark, i, 0, 9999);
343               state = S15;
344            } else if (c == '+') {
345               year = parse(value, 4, mark, i, 0, 9999);
346               nego = false;
347               state = S16;
348            } else {
349               throw bad(value, i);
350            }
351         } else if (state == S3) {
352            // S03: Found -, looking for M(S04)
353            if (digit.contains(c)) {
354               mark = i;
355               state = S4;
356               precision = ChronoField.MONTH_OF_YEAR;
357            } else {
358               throw bad(value, i);
359            }
360         } else if (state == S4) {
361            // S04: Found M, looking for M(S04)/-(S05)/T(S07)
362            if (digit.contains(c)) {
363               // Stay in S4
364            } else if (c == '-') {
365               month = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 12);
366               state = S5;
367            } else if (c == 'T') {
368               month = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 12);
369               state = S7;
370            } else if (c == 'Z') {
371               month = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 12);
372               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
373               state = S15;
374            } else if (c == '+') {
375               month = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 12);
376               nego = false;
377               state = S16;
378            } else {
379               throw bad(value, i);
380            }
381         } else if (state == S5) {
382            // S05: Found -, looking for D(S06)
383            if (digit.contains(c)) {
384               mark = i;
385               state = S6;
386               precision = ChronoField.DAY_OF_MONTH;
387            } else {
388               throw bad(value, i);
389            }
390         } else if (state == S6) {
391            // S06: Found D, looking for D(S06)/T(S07)
392            if (digit.contains(c)) {
393               // Stay in S6
394            } else if (c == 'T') {
395               day = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 31);
396               state = S7;
397            } else if (c == 'Z') {
398               day = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 31);
399               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
400               state = S15;
401            } else if (c == '+') {
402               day = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 31);
403               nego = false;
404               state = S16;
405            } else if (c == '-') {
406               day = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 1, 31);
407               nego = true;
408               state = S17;
409            } else {
410               throw bad(value, i);
411            }
412         } else if (state == S7) {
413            // S07: Found T, looking for h(S08)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17)
414            if (digit.contains(c)) {
415               mark = i;
416               state = S8;
417               precision = ChronoField.HOUR_OF_DAY;
418            } else if (c == 'Z') {
419               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
420               if (timeOnly) {
421                  precision = ChronoField.HOUR_OF_DAY;
422               }
423               state = S15;
424            } else if (c == '+') {
425               nego = false;
426               if (timeOnly) {
427                  precision = ChronoField.HOUR_OF_DAY;
428               }
429               state = S16;
430            } else if (c == '-') {
431               nego = true;
432               if (timeOnly) {
433                  precision = ChronoField.HOUR_OF_DAY;
434               }
435               state = S17;
436            } else {
437               throw bad(value, i);
438            }
439         } else if (state == S8) {
440            // S08: Found h, looking for h(S08)/:(S09)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17)
441            if (digit.contains(c)) {
442               // Stay in S8
443            } else if (c == ':') {
444               hour = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 23);
445               state = S9;
446            } else if (c == 'Z') {
447               hour = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 23);
448               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
449               state = S15;
450            } else if (c == '+') {
451               hour = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 23);
452               nego = false;
453               state = S16;
454            } else if (c == '-') {
455               hour = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 23);
456               nego = true;
457               state = S17;
458            } else {
459               throw bad(value, i);
460            }
461         } else if (state == S9) {
462            // S09: Found :, looking for m(S10)
463            if (digit.contains(c)) {
464               mark = i;
465               state = S10;
466               precision = ChronoField.MINUTE_OF_HOUR;
467            } else {
468               throw bad(value, i);
469            }
470         } else if (state == S10) {
471            // S10: Found m, looking for m(S10)/:(S11)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17)
472            if (digit.contains(c)) {
473               // Stay in S10
474            } else if (c == ':') {
475               minute = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
476               state = S11;
477            } else if (c == 'Z') {
478               minute = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
479               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
480               state = S15;
481            } else if (c == '+') {
482               minute = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
483               nego = false;
484               state = S16;
485            } else if (c == '-') {
486               minute = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
487               nego = true;
488               state = S17;
489            } else {
490               throw bad(value, i);
491            }
492         } else if (state == S11) {
493            // S11: Found :, looking for s(S12)
494            if (digit.contains(c)) {
495               mark = i;
496               state = S12;
497               precision = ChronoField.SECOND_OF_MINUTE;
498            } else {
499               throw bad(value, i);
500            }
501         } else if (state == S12) {
502            // S12: Found s, looking for s(S12)/.(S13)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17)
503            if (digit.contains(c)) {
504               // Stay in S12
505         } else if (c == '.' || c == ',') {
506            second = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
507            state = S13;
508            // Precision will be set based on number of fractional digits
509            } else if (c == 'Z') {
510               second = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
511               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
512               state = S15;
513            } else if (c == '+') {
514               second = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
515               nego = false;
516               state = S16;
517            } else if (c == '-') {
518               second = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 59);
519               nego = true;
520               state = S17;
521            } else {
522               throw bad(value, i);
523            }
524         } else if (state == S13) {
525            // S13: Found . or ,, looking for S(S14)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17)
526            if (digit.contains(c)) {
527               mark = i;
528               state = S14;
529            } else if (c == 'Z') {
530               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
531               state = S15;
532            } else if (c == '+') {
533               nego = false;
534               state = S16;
535            } else if (c == '-') {
536               nego = true;
537               state = S17;
538            } else {
539               throw bad(value, i);
540            }
541         } else if (state == S14) {
542            // S14: Found S, looking for S(S14)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17)
543            if (digit.contains(c)) {
544               // Stay in S14
545            } else if (c == 'Z') {
546               nanos = parseNanos(value, mark, i);
547               zoneId = ZoneId.of("Z");
548               // Set precision based on number of fractional digits: 1-3 = milliseconds, 4-9 = nanoseconds
549               var digitCount = i - mark;
550               precision = (digitCount <= 3) ? ChronoField.MILLI_OF_SECOND : ChronoField.NANO_OF_SECOND;
551               state = S15;
552            } else if (c == '+') {
553               nanos = parseNanos(value, mark, i);
554               // Set precision based on number of fractional digits: 1-3 = milliseconds, 4-9 = nanoseconds
555               var digitCount = i - mark;
556               precision = (digitCount <= 3) ? ChronoField.MILLI_OF_SECOND : ChronoField.NANO_OF_SECOND;
557               nego = false;
558               state = S16;
559            } else if (c == '-') {
560               nanos = parseNanos(value, mark, i);
561               // Set precision based on number of fractional digits: 1-3 = milliseconds, 4-9 = nanoseconds
562               var digitCount = i - mark;
563               precision = (digitCount <= 3) ? ChronoField.MILLI_OF_SECOND : ChronoField.NANO_OF_SECOND;
564               nego = true;
565               state = S17;
566            } else {
567               throw bad(value, i);
568            }
569         } else if (state == S15) {
570            // Shouldn't find anything after Z
571            throw bad(value, i);
572         } else if (state == S16) {
573            // S16: Found +, looking for oh(S18)
574            if (digit.contains(c)) {
575               mark = i;
576               state = S18;
577            } else {
578               throw bad(value, i);
579            }
580         } else if (state == S17) {
581            // S17: Found -, looking for oh(S18)
582            if (digit.contains(c)) {
583               mark = i;
584               state = S18;
585            } else {
586               throw bad(value, i);
587            }
588         } else if (state == S18) {
589            // S18: Found oh, looking for oh(S18)/:(S19)/end
590            if (digit.contains(c)) {
591               // Stay in S18
592            } else if (c == ':') {
593               ohour = parse(value, 2, mark, i, 0, 18);
594               state = S19;
595            } else {
596               throw bad(value, i);
597            }
598            // If we reach end of string, ohour is complete (2 digits)
599         } else if (state == S19) {
600            // S19: Found :, looking for om(S20)
601            if (digit.contains(c)) {
602               mark = i;
603               state = S20;
604            } else {
605               throw bad(value, i);
606            }
607         } else /* (state == S20) */ {
608            // S20: Found om, looking for om(S20)
609            if (digit.contains(c)) {
610               // Stay in S20
611            } else {
612               throw bad(value, i);
613            }
614         }
615      }
616
617      var end = value.length(); // end is exclusive (one past last character)
618      if (state.isAny(S1, S3, S5, S7, S9, S11, S13, S16, S17, S19)) {
619         throw bad(value, end - 1);
620      } else if (state == S2) {
621         // S02: Found Y, looking for Y(S02)/-(S03)/T(S07).
622         year = parse(value, 4, mark, end, 0, 9999);
623         precision = ChronoField.YEAR;
624      } else if (state == S4) {
625         // S04: Found M, looking for M(S04)/-(S05)/T(S07).
626         month = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 1, 12);
627         precision = ChronoField.MONTH_OF_YEAR;
628      } else if (state == S6) {
629         // S06  Found D, looking for D(S06)/T(S07).
630         day = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 1, 31);
631         precision = ChronoField.DAY_OF_MONTH;
632      } else if (state == S8) {
633         // S08: Found h, looking for h(S08)/:(S09)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
634         hour = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 0, 23);
635         precision = ChronoField.HOUR_OF_DAY;
636      } else if (state == S10) {
637         // S10: Found m, looking for m(S10)/:(S11)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
638         minute = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 0, 59);
639         precision = ChronoField.MINUTE_OF_HOUR;
640      } else if (state == S12) {
641         // S12: Found s, looking for s(S12)/.(S13)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
642         second = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 0, 59);
643         precision = ChronoField.SECOND_OF_MINUTE;
644      } else if (state == S14) {
645         // S14: Found S, looking for S(S14)/Z(S15)/+(S16)/-(S17).
646         nanos = parseNanos(value, mark, end);
647         // Set precision based on number of digits: 1-3 = milliseconds, 4-9 = nanoseconds
648         var digitCount = end - mark;
649         precision = (digitCount <= 3) ? ChronoField.MILLI_OF_SECOND : ChronoField.NANO_OF_SECOND;
650      } else if (state == S15) {
651         // S15: Found Z.
652      } else if (state == S18) {
653         // S18: Found oh, looking for oh(S18)/:(S19).
654         // Check if we have 2 digits (+hh) or 4 digits (+hhmm)
655         if (end - mark == 2) {
656            ohour = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 0, 18);
657         } else if (end - mark == 4) {
658            // +hhmm format: parse hours from mark to mark+2, minutes from mark+2 to end
659            ohour = parse(value, 2, mark, mark + 2, 0, 18);
660            ominute = parse(value, 2, mark + 2, end, 0, 59);
661         } else {
662            throw bad(value, mark);
663         }
664      } else /* (state == S20) */ {
665         // S20: Found om, looking for om(S20).
666         ominute = parse(value, 2, mark, end, 0, 59);
667      }
668
669      // Build ZoneId if we have offset information
670      if (zoneId == null && ohour >= 0) {
671         if (ominute >= 0) {
672            // If negative offset, both hours and minutes must be negative
673            var offset = ZoneOffset.ofHoursMinutes(nego ? -ohour : ohour, nego ? -ominute : ominute);
674            zoneId = offset;
675         } else {
676            var offset = ZoneOffset.ofHours(nego ? -ohour : ohour);
677            zoneId = offset;
678         }
679      }
680
681      // Use provided default zone if no zone specified, otherwise use the clock's zone
682      if (zoneId == null) {
683         zoneId = defaultZoneId != null ? defaultZoneId : clock.getZone();
684      }
685
686      // Construct ZonedDateTime from parsed values
687      // Default values for missing components
688      // For time-only formats (started with "T"), use current date
689      // For date formats, default to 1/1/1
690      if (timeOnly) {
691         // Time-only format: use current year/month/day as read from the clock in the resolved zone
692         var now = ZonedDateTime.now(clock.withZone(zoneId));
693         year = now.getYear();
694         month = now.getMonthValue();
695         day = now.getDayOfMonth();
696      }
697
698      var localDateTime = LocalDateTime.of(year, month, day, hour, minute, second);
699      if (nanos > 0) {
700         localDateTime = localDateTime.plusNanos(nanos);
701      }
702
703      var zdt = ZonedDateTime.of(localDateTime, zoneId);
704
705      // Return GranularZonedDateTime with the determined precision
706      return new GranularZonedDateTime(zdt, precision);
707   }
708
709   /**
710    * Creates a GranularZonedDateTime from a ZonedDateTime with the specified precision.
711    *
712    * <p>
713    * This is the most direct way to create a GranularZonedDateTime when you already have
714    * a ZonedDateTime and want to specify its precision.
715    *
716    * @param date The ZonedDateTime value.
717    * @param precision The precision of the time value.
718    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime instance.
719    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if date or precision is null.
720    */
721   public static GranularZonedDateTime of(ZonedDateTime date, ChronoField precision) {
722      return new GranularZonedDateTime(date, precision);
723   }
724
725
726   private static DateTimeParseException bad(String s, int pos) {
727      return new DateTimeParseException("Invalid ISO8601 timestamp", s, pos);
728   }
729
730   private static int parse(String s, int chars, int pos, int end, int min, int max) {
731      if (end-pos != chars) throw bad(s, pos);
732      var i = Integer.parseInt(s, pos, end, 10);
733      if (i < min || i > max) throw bad(s, pos);
734      return i;
735   }
736
737   private static int parseNanos(String s, int pos, int end) {
738      var len = end - pos; // Length of the substring being parsed
739      if (len > 9) {
740         throw bad(s, pos);
741      }
742      var n = Integer.parseInt(s, pos, end, 10);
743      // Convert to nanoseconds based on number of digits
744      // 1 digit = hundreds of milliseconds, 2 = tens, 3 = milliseconds, etc.
745      if (len == 1) return n * 100000000;
746      if (len == 2) return n * 10000000;
747      if (len == 3) return n * 1000000;
748      if (len == 4) return n * 100000;
749      if (len == 5) return n * 10000;
750      if (len == 6) return n * 1000;
751      if (len == 7) return n * 100;
752      if (len == 8) return n * 10;
753      return n;
754   }
755
756   /**
757    * Converts a ChronoField to its corresponding ChronoUnit.
758    *
759    * <p>
760    * This method provides a mapping from date/time fields to time units.
761    * Not all ChronoField values have direct ChronoUnit equivalents.
762    *
763    * <p>
764    * Supported fields:
765    * <ul>
766    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#YEAR} → {@link ChronoUnit#YEARS}
767    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MONTH_OF_YEAR} → {@link ChronoUnit#MONTHS}
768    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#DAY_OF_MONTH} → {@link ChronoUnit#DAYS}
769    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#HOUR_OF_DAY} → {@link ChronoUnit#HOURS}
770    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MINUTE_OF_HOUR} → {@link ChronoUnit#MINUTES}
771    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#SECOND_OF_MINUTE} → {@link ChronoUnit#SECONDS}
772    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MILLI_OF_SECOND} → {@link ChronoUnit#MILLIS}
773    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#NANO_OF_SECOND} → {@link ChronoUnit#NANOS}
774    * </ul>
775    *
776    * @param field The ChronoField to convert
777    * @return The corresponding ChronoUnit, or null if no direct mapping exists
778    */
779   private static ChronoUnit toChronoUnit(ChronoField field) {
780      return switch (field) {
781         case YEAR -> ChronoUnit.YEARS;
782         case MONTH_OF_YEAR -> ChronoUnit.MONTHS;
783         case DAY_OF_MONTH -> ChronoUnit.DAYS;
784         case HOUR_OF_DAY -> ChronoUnit.HOURS;
785         case MINUTE_OF_HOUR -> ChronoUnit.MINUTES;
786         case SECOND_OF_MINUTE -> ChronoUnit.SECONDS;
787         case MILLI_OF_SECOND -> ChronoUnit.MILLIS;
788         case NANO_OF_SECOND -> ChronoUnit.NANOS;
789         default -> null;
790      };
791   }
792
793   /** The ZonedDateTime value */
794   public final ZonedDateTime zdt;
795
796   /** The precision of this time value */
797   public final ChronoField precision;
798
799   /**
800    * Constructor.
801    *
802    * @param zdt The ZonedDateTime value.
803    * @param precision The precision of this time value.
804    */
805   public GranularZonedDateTime(ZonedDateTime zdt, ChronoField precision) {
806      this.zdt = zdt;
807      this.precision = precision;
808   }
809
810   /**
811    * Creates a copy of this object.
812    *
813    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime with the same values.
814    */
815   public GranularZonedDateTime copy() {
816      return new GranularZonedDateTime(zdt, precision);
817   }
818
819   /**
820    * Returns the ZonedDateTime value.
821    *
822    * @return The ZonedDateTime value.
823    */
824   public ZonedDateTime getZonedDateTime() { return zdt; }
825
826   /**
827    * Returns the precision of this time value.
828    *
829    * <p>
830    * The precision indicates the finest granularity of the time value, which determines
831    * how the value was parsed or created. For example:
832    * <ul>
833    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#YEAR} - Year precision (e.g., "2011")
834    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MONTH_OF_YEAR} - Month precision (e.g., "2011-01")
835    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#DAY_OF_MONTH} - Day precision (e.g., "2011-01-15")
836    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#HOUR_OF_DAY} - Hour precision (e.g., "2011-01-15T12")
837    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MINUTE_OF_HOUR} - Minute precision (e.g., "2011-01-15T12:30")
838    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#SECOND_OF_MINUTE} - Second precision (e.g., "2011-01-15T12:30:45")
839    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MILLI_OF_SECOND} - Millisecond precision (e.g., "2011-01-15T12:30:45.123")
840    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#NANO_OF_SECOND} - Nanosecond precision (e.g., "2011-01-15T12:30:45.123456789")
841    * </ul>
842    *
843    * @return The precision of this time value.
844    */
845   public ChronoField getPrecision() { return precision; }
846
847   @Override
848   public String toString() {
849      return zdt.format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME) + "(" + precision + ")";
850   }
851
852   /**
853    * Rolls this time value by the specified amount using the specified field.
854    *
855    * <p>
856    * This method creates a new GranularZonedDateTime by adding the specified amount to the
857    * specified field. The precision of the returned object remains the same as this object.
858    *
859    * <h5 class='section'>Supported Fields:</h5>
860    * <ul>
861    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#YEAR}
862    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MONTH_OF_YEAR}
863    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#DAY_OF_MONTH}
864    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#HOUR_OF_DAY}
865    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MINUTE_OF_HOUR}
866    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#SECOND_OF_MINUTE}
867    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#MILLI_OF_SECOND}
868    *    <li>{@link ChronoField#NANO_OF_SECOND}
869    * </ul>
870    *
871    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
872    * <p class='bjava'>
873    *    <jc>// Create a datetime with hour precision</jc>
874    *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"2011-01-15T12Z"</js>);
875    *    <jc>// Roll forward by 2 hours</jc>
876    *    <jv>gdt</jv> = <jv>gdt</jv>.<jsm>roll</jsm>(<jv>ChronoField</jv>.<jf>HOUR_OF_DAY</jf>, 2);
877    *    <jc>// Result: 2011-01-15T14:00:00Z (precision still HOUR_OF_DAY)</jc>
878    * </p>
879    *
880    * @param field The field to roll by. Must be one of the supported fields listed above.
881    * @param amount The amount to roll by. Positive values roll forward, negative values roll backward.
882    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime with the rolled value.
883    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the field is not supported.
884    */
885   public GranularZonedDateTime roll(ChronoField field, int amount) {
886      var unit = toChronoUnit(field);
887      assertArg(unit != null, "Unsupported roll field: {0}", field);
888      var newZdt = zdt.plus(amount, unit);
889      return new GranularZonedDateTime(newZdt, precision);
890   }
891
892   /**
893    * Rolls this time value by the specified amount using the current precision.
894    *
895    * <p>
896    * This is a convenience method that calls {@link #roll(ChronoField, int)} using this
897    * object's precision field.
898    *
899    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
900    * <p class='bjava'>
901    *    <jc>// Create a datetime with year precision</jc>
902    *    GranularZonedDateTime <jv>gdt</jv> = GranularZonedDateTime.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"2011"</js>);
903    *    <jc>// Roll forward by 1 (using YEAR precision)</jc>
904    *    <jv>gdt</jv> = <jv>gdt</jv>.<jsm>roll</jsm>(1);
905    *    <jc>// Result: 2012-01-01T00:00:00 (precision still YEAR)</jc>
906    * </p>
907    *
908    * @param amount The amount to roll by. Positive values roll forward, negative values roll backward.
909    * @return A new GranularZonedDateTime with the rolled value.
910    */
911   public GranularZonedDateTime roll(int amount) {
912      return roll(precision, amount);
913   }
914}
